Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, lets list all activities: - Initiate Request - Verify Documents - Call Customer - Submit Additional - Approve Request - Adjust Account Now, look at each case: C001: 1. Initiate Request 2. Verify Documents first time 3. Call Customer first time 4. Submit Additional 5. Verify Documents second time 6. Approve Request

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Activities that appear multiple times within the same case: - Verify Documents appears twice in C001 and C003, once in C002. - Call Customer appears twice in C002 and C003? Actually in C003 it appears once? Wait, in C003: Call Customer appears once at step 3. In C002 it appears twice. In C001 it appears once.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now, interpret context: For Verify Documents: - In C001: first verification occurs after initiating request, context says Agent checks identity documents. Then later, after customer submits additional documents, there is another verification: Agent re-checks documents now that ID scan is provided. So the first verification is initial identity verification, and the second is re-verification after additional documents. - In C002: only one verification, but context: Identity verification. So its initial verification. - In C003: first verification: Physical verification of ID. Then later, after adjusting account, there is another verification: Re-verify docs after account adjustment. So again, first is initial verification, second is re-verification. So for Verify Documents, we can distinguish between: - Initial Verification first occurrence in the case - Re-verification after some action like submitting additional or adjusting account

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly, for Call Customer: - In C001: first call: Verification incomplete, agent calls to request missing docs. So its a call to request missing documents. - In C002: first call: Outbound call to clarify account discrepancy. Then second call: Follow-up call to confirm adjustments made. So first call is for clarification, second is confirmation. - In C003: first call: Customer stepped out. Officer calls to have them return. So its a call to recall customer to the branch.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Other activities: - Initiate Request appears only once per case. - Submit Additional appears only in C001. - Adjust Account appears in C002 and C003. - Approve Request appears only once per case.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now, we need to rename the recurring activities to reflect their context. Lets propose names: For Verify Documents: - First occurrence: Verify Documents - Initial or Initial Document Verification - Second occurrence: Verify Documents - Re-verification or Re-verify Documents But note: In C002, there is only one verification, so it is initial. So we can keep it as Verify Documents - Initial for the first occurrence in each case, and Verify Documents - Re-verification for the second occurrence.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For Call Customer: We have multiple contexts. Lets list: - C001: Call to request missing documents. So: Call Customer - Request Missing Docs - C002 first call: Call to clarify discrepancy. So: Call Customer - Clarify Discrepancy - C002 second call: Call to confirm adjustments. So: Call Customer - Confirm Adjustments - C003: Call to recall customer. So: Call Customer - Recall Customer

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets assign new activity names: For each row, we will replace the activity name according to the context. C001: 1. Initiate Request - Initiate Request 2. Verify Documents first - Verify Documents - Initial 3. Call Customer first - Call Customer - Request Missing Docs 4. Submit Additional - Submit Additional 5. Verify Documents second - Verify Documents - Re-verification 6. Approve Request - Approve Request C002: 1. Initiate Request - Initiate Request 2. Verify Documents first - Verify Documents - Initial 3. Call Customer first - Call Customer - Clarify Discrepancy 4. Adjust Account - Adjust Account 5. Call Customer second - Call Customer - Confirm Adjustments 6. Approve Request - Approve Request C003: 1. Initiate Request - Initiate Request 2. Verify Documents first - Verify Documents - Initial 3. Call Customer first - Call Customer - Recall Customer 4. Adjust Account - Adjust Account 5. Verify Documents second - Verify Documents - Re-verification 6. Approve Request - Approve Request

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Justification: - For Verify Documents: The first occurrence is always the initial identity verification, while the second occurrence happens after some corrective action like submitting additional documents or adjusting account and is a re-verification. So we distinguish by Initial and Re-verification. - For Call Customer: The purpose of the call varies: in C001, its to request missing documents; in C002, first call is to clarify a discrepancy, and second call is to confirm adjustments; in C003, its to recall the customer who stepped out. So we add a suffix indicating the specific purpose. Other activities are unambiguous and remain unchanged.
